description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, bulging body constructed from vertical wooden staves. The staves are tightly bound by four dark metal hoops, two near the top and bottom edges and two at the midsection, providing structural reinforcement. The wood appears weathered and aged, with visible grain, knots, and some discoloration, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, with the top surface flush and closed, indicating the barrel is sealed. The metal hoops show signs of wear and oxidation, adding to the rustic appearance. This type of barrel is commonly used for storage or transport of liquids or dry goods in urban, industrial, or historical settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, bulging sides, and a sealed wooden top, showing signs of age and outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view of the barrel showing the full curvature of the staves and all four metal hoops, with the wooden top clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the wood grain texture and the alignment of the metal hoops, with the top surface still prominent.
description_view_2: Another side view similar to the front, highlighting the consistent weathering and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the same structural features and the closed wooden top, with minor variations in wood grain and coloration.
